body:
## Summary
- Allow CONTRIBUTOR author associations by default for public repositories.
- Document GitHub author_association limitations for public org repos and how to tighten defaults with AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY.
- Update tests/docs to match the new public default.

## Verification
- Not run per maintainer request.

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 5 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Behavior change is reflected across docs: `.agent/docs/access-policy.md`, `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`, `.agent/docs/overview/quick-start.md`, and `README.md`. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | Implementation reuses existing access-policy path by adding `CONTRIBUTOR` to `DEFAULT_PUBLIC_ALLOWED_ASSOCIATIONS` in `.agent/src/access-policy.ts`; no parallel/new mechanism introduced. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | Doc edits are narrative and policy-focused; no file inventory/table anti-pattern introduced. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | Diff is tightly scoped to requested behavior: one policy constant, one targeted triage test update, and aligned docs text only. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Smallest viable change: default allowlist update plus expectation flip in `.agent/src/__tests__/triage.test.ts` without extra architecture or branching logic. |

## Notes

- No parent issue/plan references were found in the PR metadata/body/comments, so rubric scoring was based on the live diff and changed docs/tests.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** Selected process rubrics like `default-merge-strategy`, `orchestrator-one-shot-followup-default`, and `surface-stateful-failures` are not meaningfully applicable to this PR’s scope (policy default + docs/tests alignment).

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- This PR updates Sepo’s default public-repo access policy to allow `CONTRIBUTOR`, matching private-repo defaults and addressing GitHub `author_association` visibility limits for public org repos.
- The implementation is intentionally small: add `CONTRIBUTOR` to the public default allowlist and align tests/docs.
- Documentation updates are broad and consistent across the policy doc, architecture docs, quick start, and README, including guidance for tightening access via `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY`.
- No parent issue/tracking-plan linkage was found in the PR body, comments, or review metadata, so evaluation is based on the PR diff and existing policy model.

## Review
- The behavior change is correct and documentation is mostly well-aligned. One test now appears redundant under the new default and should be repurposed to preserve meaningful coverage.
- I posted one inline comment on the changed test block: https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/pull/18#discussion_r3156020828

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Redundant public override test no longer validates override behavior | WARNING | After allowing `CONTRIBUTOR` by default in public repos, the existing “widen public repo access” case effectively tests default behavior, not route override semantics. Replace it with a narrowing override case to verify the documented hardening path. |
| Minor wording drift in access-policy docs | INFO | The “public repositories also allow …” phrasing now implies a distinction that no longer exists since private/public defaults are identical. Editorial cleanup only. |

## Progress
- Public default behavior change is implemented in [`access-policy.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/e1759ff6fe0f64550e770a4baed28c07d19fb05b/.agent/src/access-policy.ts).
- Primary test expectation was updated in [`triage.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/e1759ff6fe0f64550e770a4baed28c07d19fb05b/.agent/src/__tests__/triage.test.ts).
- Docs are in sync across README + `.agent/docs/*`.
- Reviewer disagreement noted: one review marked SHIP, another flagged the redundant test as a warning; synthesis treats this as unresolved test-coverage risk.

## Issue Details
<details>
<summary>Redundant public override test no longer validates override behavior</summary>

**Cause:**  
`CONTRIBUTOR` is now in the default public allowlist, so a route override that adds `CONTRIBUTOR` does not change effective access.

**Candidate solutions:**  
- Replace the “widen public repo access” case with a narrowing override test (for example, remove `CONTRIBUTOR` for `fix-pr` and assert `unsupported`).
- Keep the updated default-behavior test as-is, and add the narrowing test as complementary coverage.

**Comments:**  
This is the only unresolved actionable finding across reviews; inline feedback was posted on the PR diff.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step
`FIX_PR`: There is one concrete, safe branch change (test coverage correction) that can be handled by an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items
- [ ] Replace or repurpose the public “widen override” test to validate a narrowing `AGENT_ACCESS_POLICY` route override (excluding `CONTRIBUTOR`) in public repos.

## Rubrics Update

No changes were committed to `agent/rubrics` from PR #18.

I’ve completed trust filtering and existing-rubric dedup checks: PR #18 conversation only contains bot-authored comments/reviews, and the PR author is `CONTRIBUTOR` with no explicit durable rubric request in-thread. I’m finalizing with `no rubric changes`.no rubric changes

I reviewed PR #18 history and trust signals, including:
- `gh pr view` metadata + files/reviews
- issue comments, PR review comments, and reviews
- repo owner/collaborator metadata
- existing rubrics under `/home/runner/work/_temp/agent-rubrics/rubrics/`

Why no changes:
- PR conversation content is bot-authored (`sepo-agent-app[bot]`, association `NONE`), which is advisory-only for rubric learning.
- The human actor (`lolipopshock`) appears as PR author/merger with `author_association: CONTRIBUTOR`; there was no explicit durable “future agents should …” preference authored by a trusted contributor in the PR conversation.
- Existing active rubrics already cover the themes present (simple/surgical changes, docs sync), so adding from this thread would be duplicate or one-off.

title: Allow public contributors by default
